title: Podman commands can hang for minutes
body:
### Issue Description

Running a simple `podman ps` with no containers running can occasionally take minutes to respond. The only resolution seems to be `podman machine stop` then `podman machine start`, but this still is sometimes inconsistent.  Generally when this slow down is occurring any command run will take a long time to respond.

I've recreated my podman machine many times, using command `podman machine init --rootful --memory 8192 --cpus 4`

Can you let me know of some debug commands I can use to provide more diagnostics. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Issue a  Podman command
2. See slow response.

### Describe the results you received

Slow response.

### Describe the results you expected

Immediate response

### podman info output

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @JDUNNIN !

@benoitf have you seen this before?
@vrothberg no I have not seen this before !

OK, so I have some more debug information which may help - I think this scenario relates to a QEMU window that pops up when I start Podman machine with debug enabled. See terminal command and output:
```
> podman machine stop && podman machine start --log-level debug

INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug

[/usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.5.1/libexec/podman/gvproxy -listen-qemu unix:///var/folders/y8/mhsj4wpn0lz9_jyrbzh93ghc0000gn/T/podman/qmp_podman-machine-default.sock -pid-file /var/folders/y8/mhsj4wpn0lz9_jyrbzh93ghc0000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default_proxy.pid -ssh-port 50611 -forward-sock /Users/jed/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman.sock -forward-dest /run/podman/podman.sock -forward-user root -forward-identity /Users/jed/.ssh/podman-machine-default --debug]
DEBU[0000] qemu cmd: [/usr/local/bin/qemu-system-x86_64 -m 8192 -smp 4 -fw_cfg name=opt/com.coreos/config,file=/Users/jed/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.ign -qmp unix:/var/folders/y8/mhsj4wpn0lz9_jyrbzh93ghc0000gn/T/podman/qmp_podman-machine-default.sock,server=on,wait=off -netdev socket,id=vlan,fd=3 -device virtio-net-pci,netdev=vlan,mac=5a:94:ef:e4:0c:ee -device virtio-serial -chardev socket,path=/var/folders/y8/mhsj4wpn0lz9_jyrbzh93ghc0000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default_ready.sock,server=on,wait=off,id=apodman-machine-default_ready -device virtserialport,chardev=apodman-machine-default_ready,name=org.fedoraproject.port.0 -pidfile /var/folders/y8/mhsj4wpn0lz9_jyrbzh93ghc0000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default_vm.pid -machine q35,accel=hvf:tcg -cpu host -virtfs local,path=/Users,mount_tag=vol0,security_model=none -virtfs local,path=/private,mount_tag=vol1,security_model=none -virtfs local,path=/var/folders,mount_tag=vol2,security_model=none -drive if=virtio,file=/Users/jed/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-38.20230625.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2]

DEBU[0012] Executing: ssh [-i /Users/jed/.ssh/podman-machine-default -p 50611 core@localhost -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o LogLevel=ERROR -o SetEnv=LC_ALL= -q -- sudo chattr -i / ; sudo mkdir -p /Users ; sudo chattr +i / ;] 
DEBU[0014] Executing: ssh [-i /Users/jed/.ssh/podman-machine-default -p 50611 core@localhost -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o LogLevel=ERROR -o SetEnv=LC_ALL= -q -- sudo mount -t 9p -o trans=virtio vol0 /Users -o version=9p2000.L,msize=131072]

DEBU[0014] Executing: ssh [-i /Users/jed/.ssh/podman-machine-default -p 50611 core@localhost -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o LogLevel=ERROR -o SetEnv=LC_ALL= -q -- sudo mount -t 9p -o trans=virtio vol1 /private -o version=9p2000.L,msize=131072]

Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.

```

Following this I noticed I had another terminal window which had been started with title `QEMU` with this content that is prompting for a localhost login.

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/25950902/d78d018b-b5ab-41ba-a64c-3100e1ddbb61)

I found that there was a massive slow down in responses while this is active, though after leaving for many minutes I can get reliable responses. If I quit this `QEMU` window then my `podman ps` command then fails with error `Error: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:50611: connect: connection refused`. This leads to me to have to restart the podman machine.

I assume I am getting the `QEMU` window because of the `--log-level debug`. 

Edit (few hours later): It does seem I can get the slow down without this window open so may just be totally unrelated.

Another symptom I've observed is that once I notice a freeze and start trying to do other Podman commands in other terminals etc, all will be frozen until suddenly they all complete and return at once. Suggesting a process finishes or a lock clears, unblocking them all.

The window is coming up because of the log-level being set.  If we are trying to solve boot problems, that is how we get a console view.  It should be totally unrelated.  Your theory on locking and blocking is probably closer but it could also be a slow connection.

If you perform a `podman machine ssh`, is the login quick?  If not, then the problem would seemingly be about getting the ssh connection itself.

If the connection is quick, does top or ps reveal anything that is running unusually or a podman command that is somehow tying things up?
Next time I spot a slow down I will try @baude - fairly certain this command also can hang.

At least sometimes the slow down seems to coincide with a test starting a container (using TestContainers). During this 2m startup my `podman ps` hung but I could use `podman machine ssh` (though it was slow to load the terminal prompt. This part loaded immediately:

```
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 38.20230709.2.0
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

Last login: Mon Jul 17 13:53:35 2023 from 192.168.127.1
```
But this line took 10+ seconds to display:
```
[root@localhost ~]#
```

On my local machine while my `podman ps` command was waiting, I issued `ps -ef | grep podman` a couple of times.
1:


Not sure if any of this helps, but at least some diagnostics!

I have the same problem. It's only happening in a rootful environment. It's not happening in rootless.

```
podman version 4.6.0
macOS Ventura 13.4.1, amd64
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Mac M1. When I run podman (default overlay driver) build commands I get a huge spike of traffic for gvproxy. It happens even on tiny builds like `FROM alpint`(40mb). I have struggled to identify what exactly is causing so much traffic in gvproxy but to no luck so far. If I run this command again you'd expect the cache to kick in. Nothing. Still the same insanely long wait. Always the same pattern of traffic in gvproxy. On every single build. And what is even more annoying is that for some time this worked fine on my machine until I had a sleep cycle toss everything out of the window... It does not seem to be a issue related to caching. When I run it again podman reports using cache for the build steps that involve copying files, etc. So it's something that happens before the actual build process. If I use cache verbose there's no information provided during this long wait period.
Do you have a disk size of at least 100GB free? Podman requires it `Error: new disk size must be larger than 100 GB`

For me this issue eventually resolved, maybe due to Podman updates. Currently using:
```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.0.2
API Version:  5.0.2
Go Version:   go1.22.2
Git Commit:   3304dd95b8978a8346b96b7d43134990609b3b29
Built:        Wed Apr 17 20:16:39 2024
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.0.2
API Version:  5.0.2
Go Version:   go1.21.9
Built:        Wed Apr 17 01:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
